Okay, so now we're gonna move into, this is probably my favorite part. As I was going through this whole discovery phase and like looking at all these patterns and stuff, the third piece here is by far of all the stuff, like the one that I've geeked out the most about, got more excited. I feel like it's the piece that a lot of people miss. A lot of people are good at becoming the attractive character. A lot of people are good at causing culture building and things like that. But this is where I think the majority of the mistakes are happening, especially working with inner circle members. After I become more aware of this, this is like the problem with most people's offers and most of the products. And so the third step here in how to create a mass movement is the new opportunity or the vehicle change that you're offering somebody. Okay, and so that's what we're gonna be talking about during this session. And when I started looking at everything, like you look at all of the mass movements again, this is like a big thing, like there's, and the, how do I explain this? So there's two things. There's like offers that you're trying to help people become better and there's offers where you're offering people a new opportunity, like a new thing that they can have. You look at all the mass movements and none of the mass movements were ever built upon like, hey, we're gonna help you become better at this thing. Like it's super uninspiring. And the true believer he talks about, if you look at like traditional organizations, like going to college or going to school, it's like you're trying to like get better and you move up through the ranks. You go to like a corporate, in the corporate world, like you start at the bottom and you're trying to improve your way to the top, which is super uninspiring. And movements are not built on that concept at all. It's not built on like, I'm gonna keep progressing it better and better and eventually I'll get somewhere. It's like, no, here's an entirely new opportunity I wanna offer you. Okay, so if you look at the spectrums of Hitler on one side and Christ at the other side, like Hitler came to the German people and what was his message? It was not like, hey guys, this whole thing sucks. All the issues we're having, like we should, you know, maybe we can get our, some of these, the fines and stuff and the fees we have to pay lower and let's change some things. Hitler came in and was like, no guys, like this whole thing is not true. I'm not a big history buff. So I don't know all the details behind it. He basically came in and was like, look, the treaty that they had after World War I, like that's false. This is not true and we're gonna get rid of it. I'm gonna offer you guys a new opportunity. Like we are the German people. Like this is our thing, like this is, we're not gonna make this better for everyone. We're getting rid of this old thing that we had that's holding all of us back and we're presenting a new opportunity as people got excited and they followed him. Okay, when Christ came, he didn't come back and say, okay, you guys are delivering a lot of Moses, we're gonna do a little bit better, we're gonna make it a little faster. He's like, no, the lot of Moses is done, it's gone, it's over. We're bringing you is a new covenant, a new set, we're coming back and instead of doing animal sacrifices, we're gonna be doing a broken heart and a contrite spirit. So there's his thing and this is a new opportunity. It was different than people like, wow, like I want to be part of that. Can you look fast forward to nowadays, Steve Jobs when he came out, right? He's in his stage with his black turtleneck on, he's doing his whole thing. He didn't come to people like, hey guys, I got this thing. This is a CD player. Not gonna be able to hold six CDs. We're gonna have 26 or 36. Without him making that thing better and it would have been uninspiring, no one cared. He's like, look you guys, up to this point, you gotta get CDs and all these things to be able to listen to your music, like that's gone. Now, he pulls out the iPod, you remember this? He's like, this is it, 2,500 songs done. This is a new opportunity, this is different. Every in your pocket, every, if you look at all the mass movements that are all built on that, like offering people a new opportunity. And so what's interesting though is that most of us, when we don't, we come back and we have this thing, like there's improvement and there's a new opportunity. And for whatever reason, I think it's partly because like how we've always been raised, is like we're always trying to make people better. Like I'm gonna help you to be faster or stronger or smarter or better. But as soon as you have like, for someone to agree, if you say like, I'm gonna buy my course, buy my product and it's gonna make you smarter, for them to say, I want to buy that, they by default have to admit that they're dumb. If I wanna be smarter, I have to admit that I failed. If I wanna get whatever that thing is. And so there's this internal struggle that will always happen when you're offering improvement offers. And there's a whole bunch of reasons why in the book, as you guys go through it, you'll see some of the core ones in here. Excuse me. First one is the improvement is hard, right? How many of you guys wanna come better at something? Like it's hard, like you've done it before. Like, and so like to try to do the same thing, like I'm gonna try to do better, like it's just, it's painful. Like in our minds, we know like what a pain it has to go through, right? Like I'll use weight loss, for example. If I've tried to lose weight 10 different times, and someone's like, hey, we're gonna lose weight together, we're gonna do it, but it's like the same thing, like we're gonna do it. Like I've tried so many times, I know what that pain looks like, I know I failed over and over and over again. So I have to like, get back and do it again, like come back in to do it again, it's hard. Because I can remember all of the issues that happened in the past when I tried it, so that's what you're fighting against. Or if I can say like, no, like everything you've done in the past, like that's, like it's gone, like here's this new, again, new opportunity, new thing, then you have no like perception of like that can be easy or gonna be hard. Like most of you guys became entrepreneurs, like someone presented it, like I bet half of you guys happened in a hotel room or a home party, right? And you're like, are you kidding me? Like I can get paid for this, I can tell my friends and my mom and dad and I'll get paid. Like it was this new opportunity, right? And you came in and you were so excited, but those you've done it a whole bunch of times, like now it becomes like improved, how do you become better? And it's like the new opportunity is what brought you into whatever it is you're doing right now, right? Somebody offered that opportunity to you. So, and because you didn't know yet, like I think most of you guys knew initially network marketing, how hard it was gonna be, you wouldn't have been all excited, but you don't know because it's a new thing. It's like, it's so new, like you don't, you're not even aware of the pain you're probably gonna have to go through. Like how many of you guys, was there a lot of pain for you to be able to get to this room eventually, right? Like if somebody told me like, hey Russell, you're gonna be an overnight success, it's gonna take you 12 years and you're not gonna sleep pretty much the entire time. It's gonna be really, really hard. Your friends, your family members are gonna leave you a lot of times. Like you're gonna go through bankruptcy almost three times, like all this stuff, like I'm gonna sign up for that, okay? But that's when you offer some improvement, like they're thinking about all the stuff that fell in the past and like, I don't wanna do that again. But if I'm like, dude, this whole new thing, check this out, then they have no idea what the past can look like. And so they're more likely to take that. Does that make sense? Because improvement is hard, people's mind. Second thing is that typically people that wanna improve, like they're ambitious, right? And how many people, what would be your guess if you were to take the American population? How many of those people are actually ambitious and actually want to improve and get better? Five percent. Yeah, I don't know how much, you thought I had the answer. I don't know, but it's small, right? Okay, in fact, the funny thing is, if you look at like most funnels, most value ladders, the people that end up in the very end are usually your improvement people. Like my inner circle, most of you guys wanna improve your doing, right? So it's on the back end, I don't sell it on the front end. If I was like, try to convince you guys on the front, like we're gonna make your business, like it's horrible, right? So the improvement like pops out the bottom, like you'll get the people that have high ambition, but most people are not ambitious. So if you're selling improvement, you're like 99.9, whatever that percentage of the world is, it's gonna be like, I don't want that, like that sounds nice and all, but that seems way too hard, right? So we have to understand that most people do not have ambitions. The next thing is commodity pricing. When you are selling improvement, your commodity, there's a lot of people trying to improve something, right? And so what happens when someone's a commodity, like I talked about earlier, like it drives the price down over and over and over again. So as soon as you're like, yeah, I also help people to lose weight. Oh, I also hope you like, as soon as you're in improvement, then you're grouped with everybody else trying to improve that one thing, and also in pricing drops. Where if you're a new opportunity, like I'm the only person in the world that does this, I'm the only person that can offer this, like there's no price barrier, like you can charge whatever makes sense, okay? Which is a huge thing, it's pulling that out, okay? So that's kind of the first piece to understand is every mass movement is created, the third piece of that is it has to be a new opportunity. And for a lot of you guys, this is gonna take some thinking through, like what am I offering people? Like what is this new opportunity I'm giving people? I gotta plug them into some kind of, this new vehicle, this new thing. Now, as you're going through this, the next thing that I learned is from Perry Belcher, I'd never heard this before, and when he first explained to me, at first I discounted it, and I like try to talk out of it, like five or six different times, and eventually he convinced me everywhere, I was like holy crap, he's right, I didn't even know this. And what he talked to me about was he said, that all of your decisions on why you either do something or why you don't do something is tied to that magic word, status. Will this choice I'm gonna make, is it gonna increase my status, or is it going to decrease my status? That's the conversation all of your customers heads, all day long. So you're like, hey, you should get click funnels, and they're like thinking about all these things, right? Like if I get that, and I make money, I'm gonna increase my status, that'd be awesome. What happens if I get it, I fail? My friends find out, I'm gonna decrease my status. And they're like, oh, but they got 30 guarantees, sweet, so that means my status won't, I can get my refund if I need some, back to status normal, and they're like, oh, but then maybe I can be in too comical, that'd be increase my status, that'd be awesome. And they're like, oh, but what if I'm not technical, I can't figure this out, and all of a sudden they're like, oh, decrease, and so like going through this thing back and forth, and they're trying to figure out, because they don't want, they want their status to increase, but they don't want it to decrease. It's almost scary for someone to have their status in fact, that's why there's so many people, we used to call them Sibs, serial information buyers, they'll buy everything, they won't do anything. And half the reason why is because they're scared that if they do do it, and it doesn't work, then their dream disappears, right? And there's so much fear behind that, like, ah, so they just don't try because they don't want to lose their dream. And so you have to understand that when you're making any kind of new opportunity, someone is like, this is what's driving them, is this, is status. Like, if I take this new opportunity, if I take this leap of faith into this new opportunity, what's that gonna do to my status? Okay, and so when I first heard that, I was like, that's not true, people aren't that consumed with status. So I was asking Perry, I'm like, well, what about, like, my wife, for example, like, I don't like, I wanna drive a Ferrari, like, it increases my status, like, I feel cool behind the thing, right? My wife, like, she's embarrassed of it, like, she wants, like, a mom car, that no one's gonna worry about, and he's like, well, why did she want that? I was like, cause she doesn't want people to judge her, it's like, if they judge her, what would happen? It's like, ah, it would decrease her status. It's not weird, it works on both directions, it's not just like, people wanting monetary things, it's also why people don't want monetary things, like, if I do that, like, when I moved into my new house, like, my mom was like, if you move in that big house, then guess what's gonna happen? She's like, all the people, you're gonna be like, those rich people on the mountain, and like, all these things, I'm like, mom, like, you know, like, I got all this fear, like, it's gonna decrease my status, and buy us a nice house, and my mom's like, like, I had to convince my mom that, like, I'm still gonna be normal, I'm still gonna be a nice guy, just, you know, and like, this is like, the thing that all of us are dealing with definitely, but also, like, everyone of your customers. Think about when you invested in Inner Circle, how many of you guys felt that fear of like, huh, like, $25,000, like, that's a lot of money, okay? And you're like, I'm not gonna tell anyone, because if I fail, I don't want them to decrease my status, right? That's like, part of it. I'm not even told what anyone is. Yeah. Yeah. But as soon as you crush it with your new thing, then you're gonna be like, everyone. Oh, hell, he is. Like, how smart I am. Yeah. In fact, I have a lot of friends, this is why they struggle, is they'll get an idea, and the first thing they do, they get so excited about that idea, and they call everyone, they know, like, I'm doing this, and they tell every single person on earth, and then they're like, I'm gonna go out and attempt it, and it's like, if you fail, like, you just, why'd you tell everyone already? Like, I don't tell anyone anything that's gonna happen. I'm just like, try it, and like, oh crap, I failed. Good thing nobody knew about it. Huh, huh, you know? Does that wanna do, and then when you happen, you're like, yeah, you celebrate. And so it's like, figuring out, and we're gonna talk, one big thing to say, all the stuff we're doing today, and tomorrow, you guys, is like, building blocks of stuff that we're gonna be plugging into our presentations later. So like, there's so many ways in our presentation we're weaving status into it. So I want you guys to understand all these concepts now, and like, I said, we'll be plugging them in tomorrow. But, understanding that when you're doing your presentations, you're thinking about this, every single thing, every single thing you're offering, everything you're saying, like, what's the perceived thing in their mind? Is it gonna increase their status or decrease it? And if it's gonna decrease it, what do you create to bring it back to stability, right? Like, if I'm, if I know people, if they're gonna spend $1,000 on our funnel hacks thing, if they pay money, there's an immediate decrease in status that their bank account went down. So how do I, how do I bring it back to here, okay? I offer refund policy or whatever that thing, like, I'm always thinking how do I bring it back to here and then to get them to here? So if they leave the presentation here where the only option is they've increased their status, and there's in, there's no, there's no fear anymore. If I leave them in the middle that like, ah, well, I could, I couldn't, or I'm down here, there's no way they're gonna buy. Like, I have to get them from here to here and here to here as soon as they're like, the only, like, no matter what happens, it's gonna increase my status. And if it doesn't get my money back, like, if they believe that, then they're gonna buy. Cause that's the only thing that's keeping them back. Isn't that interesting? When you understand that, and how do you weave that into all these different things? In fact, a big thing that we're trying, I'm trying to do is like the stuff that we're doing is trying to make it cool. Cause when I got into this business like 12 years ago, like, internet marketing stuff was not cool. Like, everybody made fun of me. Like, it was not a cool thing, but we're trying to make it like a cool, I guess a whole funnel hacker, like all the stuff we do is like, trying to make this cool because it increases their status. Like, people have the shirts, like, I'm part of this cult, sure, like, I have all these things, like, trying to make it a cool thing so that it's a status symbol all the time. Reason why funnel hacking live, and I have 100 people on stage, is I want them to increase their status. People see, like, I want to increase, it's all about status on both directions. So, anyway, when I realized that, I was like, oh, this is so, so exciting. So as I'm coming back, and I'm making these new opportunities, I'm thinking through that, like, what's happening to their status that they agree to follow me into this new thing? Okay, and not like on the religion side, like, if you think about that, like, when somebody, when somebody, if they're living in the world, and they decide to become Christian, like, they're stepping into this new opportunity, like, that's a lot of fear. Sometimes I lose family, my friends, like, when you're making that step, like, there's so much fear, okay? But the same thing's happening when they're buying into what you guys are doing. Like, in religion, you see, like, political part, like, I never told anyone, like, my political beliefs, because I know as soon as I, like, publicly say stuff, like, I have so much fear of, like, decrease of status, I'm just like, I'm not talking about it. And it's the same thing, like, we see it from, like, from, like, stepping into new religion, or stepping into, like, a huge political, like, we see that, but, like, you don't understand, like, your people are feeling that as well. Like, for them to say, man, I'm gonna spend $1,000 on this thing, like, they're stepping in, and they're gonna have, a lot of times, friends or family members or people that don't understand it, and there's so much fear, so it's like, how do you, how do you create something when you bring that person in, like, they, they fill it home, like, all these other things we're talking about, I'll kind of tie into it, but it increases their status, and they fill part of that. And we understand that, like, it just becomes, it becomes really powerful, and it kind of reframes how you're, how you're doing things, and how you're selling things. Come. All right, so now I'm gonna come back here to the new opportunity, so, as I was going through this, I'm like, well, how do you, like, how do you make new opportunities, like, what does that look like? And I kind of figured out there's two basic, there's two basic new opportunities you can give someone, okay, so the first one is called an opportunity switch. Okay, so right now, and we'll give this a story as well, like, the people that are, that you want to follow you, let's say they wanna lose weight, or they wanna make money, or they wanna, whatever that thing is, like, they're already trying to get the result they want in some other vehicle, some other opportunity, right? So let's just say, I wanna lose weight, so I am, I don't know, let's say I'm doing the, what's the diet? South-East, so like, I'm in the South-East, this is my world, like, this is the opportunity, the vehicle I've stepped into, so they're doing something. So you're coming, you're saying, look, your vehicle sucks, lead page sucks, and the enthusiasm soft sucks, right? Like, you're in a bad vehicle, I'm gonna switch you into a new vehicle that's gonna get you to where you actually wanna go. Because you're sitting here, you're trying to get there, and you're like, I can't get there, like, it's stuck, like, the stupid car doesn't work, the tires are just like, this is horrible, right? So you're coming to them and saying, look, that opportunity, you know, that was bad, it's wrong, whatever, you know, however you want to fluff it up if you're nicer than me, like, this is, like, come over here, this is the new opportunity, and then, now we can go on, get to the result that you're trying to get to, right? So it's an opportunity switch, okay? And typically, every business, I believe, should have one opportunity switch, where I'm switching them into, it is, what my thing. And typically, that's your front end offer, okay? I'm taking you from whatever it is you're currently doing, I'm switching you to this new opportunity, this is the new thing I want you to go into, okay? That's why a lot of my friends who've been doing this for 10 years, like, as long as I've been doing it, the biggest thing I see them doing, and I had this problem for a while as well, is they come in and they launch new product every, like, 30 days, and they're, like, switching opportunity, and then, like, everyone buys them, and they go, hey, and they switch it again, and they keep switching, and they keep, and, like, the customers, can you imagine, like, this, like, schizophrenic, they're going through, like, okay, I'm in this thing, yeah, and they're like, oh, he's talking about this, okay, I'm over here, oh, and they're like, oh, and they keep moving, and moving, and moving, and like, because of that, the customers stop losing faith, their status goes down, and they leave, they walk away. Can you notice, like, what have I been talking about every single day for the last three years, you guys? Fun. Yeah, fun, I was like, I haven't changed my message, like, this is the new opportunity, come join me, this is the new opportunity, then when you're in, so I've switched you from whatever opportunity you're trying to, you're trying to invest in real estate, you're trying to make money doing some other things, you're using different, like, whatever opportunity you're in, I'm switching you to my opportunity, this is where we're at, and then, the rest of my value ladder is opportunity stacking, so now you're in this vehicle, how can I make this better for you? Okay, I'm gonna stack an opportunity, so I'm gonna say, look, using ClickFunnels, how would you like to become certified and actually make money as a ClickFunnels consultant? This is an opportunity stack, I'm not shifting your focus, so you're already in this vehicle, here's the new opportunity, and so I'm stacking an opportunity, now you're a certified partner, okay? I'm looking at things like that, certified partner, like, you're already doing funnels, how many of you guys need funnel scripts, you can actually have sales letters, okay, I'm gonna stack this opportunity, and so I'm looking at, like, I'm not shifting people from my vehicle, but any other opportunity I'm making needs to be a stack, where it's making whatever the original vehicle, it ain't better, okay, so I'm upgrading the tires, the car, all those kind of things, okay? And so that's why I look at these kind of things, is the opportunity to switch and the opportunity to stack. And so, typically, in everybody's front end business, the first thing, so it might be a book, it might be a webinar, it might be whatever, it might be something at the ad, I mean, it could be whatever that is, but you're trying to think through, it's like, what is the current opportunity these guys are in, and what am I switching them to, right? So the first, the initial offer is a switch, okay? And then after that, I think I have an image of this, hold on, oh yeah, it's two for, I'm gonna go. Okay, so the first thing is opportunity to switch, so I'm trying, so like, let's say the expert secrets book, the dot com secrets book, the funnel hacks webinar, I'm switching them from whatever source I'm bringing them to, you switch them into this opportunity, then after that, then I'm like stacking things, okay? So these are the other things that I sell people, so we got click funnels, we got funnel scripts, fill your funnel, there are new course coming out, certified, so these are all opportunities within this opportunity, does that make sense? And then switch to the stack, okay? Now, where are you switching them from? So if I go, I'm gonna go back one slide. So this is typically happening, if you come back to what we talked about with the submarkets, okay? So you guys came here, you had your core market, your submarket, and you created your niche, okay? So this is my niche among all these other niches. So where the opportunity to switch is typically, number one is within this, here's all the other people, so I'm switching them from whatever their thing might be, from Akins diet to South Beach, or those things, I'm switching them from there to my thing, or a lot of times you can switch even out at this level, so right now they're investing in real estate, or teach them how to make money in internet marketing, so you can step a level back, does that make sense? So we sell our stuff to real estate investors, we sell our stuff to stock traders, because they're trying to get the same result, if they're in wealth, they're trying to make money, so I'm just switching there the whole thing, if they're in health, with Anthony, we came with biohacking seekers, like Anthony was initially a weight loss health coach, and we kind of stepped back and said, look, this created a new thing, it's biohacking, it's different, but he can go step back now, and anyone who's trying to get the end result of health, he can bring all those people in, he can switch from, okay, I'm trying to lose weight, I'm gonna biohack, I'm trying to do a diet to buy, like you can switch at both those two levels, okay? So that's kind of where the switches are usually happening, and so that helps, when we get to the traffic side of things, this will make more sense, because you're seeing like, where am I getting my traffic from, what step, you're getting it from here, or you can go back, back one level and get it within here, does that make sense? Okay, I don't understand this stuff with kind of complex things, but they'll make more and more sense as we keep going through these, I need you to read the book, okay? All right, so the biggest thing to understand is that, the new opportunity is a vehicle, you're putting somebody in, and the goal of that is to get them some result, right? Like that's it, and so for you guys, I want you to start thinking about that, is what's the result that these guys need, what's the result they want, and what's the vehicle I'm gonna put them into, the new opportunity, this comes back to kind of, we talked about earlier today with defining your niche, but what is that actual vehicle, what's the thing, what's the opportunity? All right, who else wants to go? Carrie, whoa, nice catch. Okay, so mine is, I'm gonna be vulnerable because I don't know the answer to this, and so I'm hoping that you guys can help me define a little bit more. So mine is a fitness and exercise program, which there's a million of them out there. But the difference between mine is, is that as I've been involved in the fitness industry for over 20 years, I can give you all of my credentials with it to impress you, but the thing is, is my personal experience, and that is, I can tell you how to lose weight in your 20s of all the struggles that you have. I can tell you how to lose weight after you have babies because I've had four of them, but the thing that's not being discussed with women is that 35 to 55 age group where you are in the thick of raising kids, you're in the thick of life, and at the same time, you wanna do something with your life. So it's just trying, and your body changes so much. It's hard to make the, hard to make it work there. Chris? So I was just looking at mine and listening to everybody else's here. I started off saying, I'm going to teach acupuncturists how to create an authentic high volume cash practice, blah, blah, blah, blah. I stuff this thing with everything they could possibly want in one sentence. But after listening to you guys, then I'm like, okay, I'm going to teach serious growth-minded acupuncturists how to successfully shift into an authentic high income, blah, blah. So it's like, it's a new opportunity. It's like where they are, their vehicle right now is they're not, they don't have a high volume, high income, time leverage practice. Stephen had kind of an epiphany he wanted to share, which I thought was really, really cool about how to structure new opportunity for some of you guys who were really stuck out. I don't know how to do that. Do you want to kind of talk about your epiphany? Yeah, absolutely. So a lot of you guys, I mean, when you think about the sub market itself, obviously there's going to be other people selling similar things to what you're doing. One of the easiest ways to create a brand new opportunity and create the niche is simply by switching up the way you deliver the same content someone else has. So you think about what Russell was saying about that really cool, sexy offer where, hey, come out and for granted, you're going to watch me build a business. And at the end of it, you're going to give it to somebody. I mean, that might be the same as what somebody else is teaching. He's merely switching up the way someone consumes it. It's a very easy, fast way to create a new opportunity. Thank you. Think about that. Everyone else is selling books. How many books have you bought? Books don't work. You got to come to my live event. Oh, everyone goes live events. It's so stupid. What we do is a mastermind. Everyone is mastermind. So that can be the switch is the delivery mechanism. I thought that was really, really cool. All right. So let's come back to here. So we talked about this, right? So here is your customer. They're currently in some kind of opportunity, right? And they're trying to get to this goal over here they want. So every business here, the result is different. They want something tied to their health, their relationships, their wealth. There's something that they're trying to get, right? This is the result. And so you're coming up to him saying basically, this opportunity you're now sucked, you're never going to get there no matter how hard you try. It's not your fault though. I want to encourage your dreams. I want to justify your failures. We're going through all the persuasion stuff. And you're saying, but this right here, this is the new opportunity you're jumping into. And this new opportunity is the key that's going to get you up here. Okay? And so that's kind of what we're doing with this whole opportunity switch. Now the problem that a lot of us as experts have because we're so smart and we know our material and our ideas so well, then we say, okay guys, this is what we're going to create for you. And like we just start like giving people what we think that they want. And sometimes you get lucky and you have success because of that. But the majority of times it's actually, it's not, the majority of times you're going to screw up what they actually want. We know they want this result, but we have to make sure we customize this in a way that they want. If you get better at the customization, the better the results are going to be and the more likely and more happy they're going to be with that. Okay? So if I'm giving this new vehicle, I'm like, what do you guys want? Like, I want a CD player. Like sweet, I'm putting a CD player in that new vehicle, whatever it might be. Okay? So that's kind of the analogy now to put it into practical terms. When I first got started in this business, some of my first coaches were Frank Kern and Ed Dale. They had a course called Underachievers. Do you guys remember that? Which was awesome for those of you who remember it. I ended up actually buying the brand from them a couple of years later because it was so cool. We haven't talked about it. We shut down that whole company probably seven or eight years ago, but I love the concept. One of the core things they taught in that was this, is that there's a three-step process to be successful online. It's so simple, yet it's like the backwards. Most of us, we come in and we're like, okay, I have this idea for this product. You create the product and then you're like, oh, it's stunning. You try to shove it down people's throats and you try to get them to buy the thing. And that's sometimes you get lucky, but most of the time that's the issue. So if you look at the system, it's step number one is find a hot market. That's what we did earlier today, making sure you're finding here's the three markets. Here's the sub market. Here's our niche within there, but we know that's a hot market based on what we figured out and talked about earlier today. The second step then is not go create a product and sell it to them. Second step is ask them what they actually want. Like what do you guys actually want? Oh, you want to do that? Cool. And then third step is then give that to them. So it's not find the market, create a product and shove it down their throats. It's find a market, ask them what they want, and then just give it to them. It's pretty simple. Guess how we came up with click funnels? Every now and then our market's like, I wish we had an easy way to build funnels. All right. Hey Todd, can you build that? Yeah, man. Sweet, let's build that and give it to them. And that was it, right? Like it's pretty much, I mean there's little details that he had to do. I don't know what it was, but it seemed easy. But we just had to ask them what they want. So this is the key is this middle step here. Ryan the Vec were a really good book called Ask. And so we're showing you guys the Met have read it. And I like the principles, but for most of us, it's way intense. Like it's, we had a higher team of people to do it for us. And it was awesome. That's what I'm talking about here. This is like a very simplified version of that. Something that all of us can implement. In fact, all of you all are going to implement that tonight. We're going to have, we're going to build one. Everyone's going to do it. It's going to be really, really fun. Okay. And so that's kind of what it is. So what an Ask campaign is, is basically when we first started doing them, they were basically landing page look like this. Okay. Now a lot of you guys already have stuff happening. For those who don't yet, what in the book we talked about this, a lot of people reading the book aren't going to have any kind of business up front. And so what I recommend in the book is for everyone is initially like we need to get results for your customers first, right? So putting on a free class or free, whatever something to like to, to get results for, for people, right? First. And so the concept is like, I'm going to put on a class. In fact, one of my friends did this when he was trying to get clients. He built a class. It was called ethical cult building. And it was a free class. Everyone can come in and he taught the course. Then from there, he got tons of clients, but he could have then gone, you know, stuff we'll talk about tomorrow and build into a whole company, but he didn't. But the concept was he had this class out there and he put it out there. He said it was free. And it was like basically asking me a question about whatever question you have. And we're going to take those questions and from there we'll build the curriculum. And it was, it was an amazing experience. I had a great time going through his, his course. This ask campaign like a couple of stories just to show you guys the, the power in it. One of my businesses, as you know, we have a company called Overcomponography. And when we first came out the idea for the company, we started putting it all together and I was assuming that like all of our customers would be men who are addicted to pornography. And so like that's who like the product started getting created towards them and like all the marketing and everything. And by that time, as I heard about these ask campaigns, I was like, okay, I'm going to do that. So we put out a page and it was really simple. All I said is like, what's your number one question about how to overcome pornography addiction? And we put it out there. We started buying, this is pre-Facebook. So we're buying Google ads to it. We were going to different parenting groups. And what was interesting in this completely transition, transformed like the direction of the entire company is that the people that started filling this form out. We're not the people who I thought I thought they're going to be men who were struggling with pornography addiction. It was wives of men who were struggling. It was mothers had kids that were struggling. Like those are the people that responded. And all the questions came in like, well, my husband struggling like, what should I do? My kids having this issue. Like, and all these questions started coming in. I was like, wow, like the people that are actually interested in this is a whole different mark. I missed the mark completely. I was so close to it. And so it transformed everything from how we built the products, how we built this. Everything changed because we figured that out. And so that's like the power in these things is like, it helps you figure out exactly what your people want. And then you just give them what they ask for. And it's like the easiest way to make money. Like it's the most simple thing in the world. You don't have to reinvent the wheel. It's just asking what they want. And so basically, let's see. I'm going to make sure we got this. So basically what we do is we're going to ask these people, like, what do you want in this vehicle? What are the things? And John's going to come up here in a few minutes and he's going to be showing a couple different ways to do this. Because it used to be we'd create a page like this and we'd drive Google ads to it or Facebook ads, whatever it is. And this method still works. But a lot of times nowadays, there's easier, like kind of covert ways to do it that are especially in Facebook that make it really, really easy. And so John's going to show you guys some of those things. My goal, though, is to get people to come through this ask campaign. And I'm going to ask them that one question. And it's simple. Just listen. I'll say it. It'll be good. Okay. I'll say it. And then it's on this page when you guys do find it. So basically this is the script. Dear friend, or hey, my name is Russell Brunson. I know your time is valuable, so I'm going to get right to the point. I need your advice. I need your advice. I'm putting the finishing touches on a new course called How to Make Money in Your Underwear. How to Biohack. How to Lose Weight. How to, like, whatever your thing is, right? Whatever your unique opportunity is. And I want to make sure I don't leave anything out. So if you let me know, your biggest question is about how to overcome weight loss, overcome whatever, overcome doggy addiction, how to make a potato gun, whatever your thing is. It could be anything. You may even think it's silly. All you have to do is type in your question in the box below and click Submit. In exchange for your advice, I'll give you free access to my new course that's happening on June 16th. This course can sell for $197 in the future, but you get special access to free to come. All you have to do is let me know your number one question. Okay? So a couple of reasons why we're doing this. The first reason, I'm going to go back and put a slide into this because I think it's important. If you want to take somebody to here, and you want to give them the result, one of the biggest problems a lot of people have is they come in like, I'm going to be an expert. I'm going to take someone up here. I know how to do it. They're like, I'm the greatest person in the world. You should come listen to me. I'll take you on this journey. But there's no proof that they actually know what they're talking about. So I got two stories to tell about this. The first one was, I have a friend. He was a high schooler at the time. His name's Alec Jetzel. Alec wanted to get a job. This is probably five or six years ago. So during summer, he went and he applied at a whole bunch of different places. Everyone just basically turned him down. So he didn't get a job. He's like, oh, I'm a talented kid. I work hard. They should give me a job. So eventually he went down to the mall and he found his favorite clothing store. He's like, I'm going to get a job there. And he walked in and he was going to apply and all that stuff. He's just like, man, they're not going to hire me. So he walked up to one of the managers. He's like, hey, I know this store's kind of messed up. Clothes are everywhere. Do you mind? I'm just kind of like OCD a little bit. Do you mind if I just clean things up? And the manager's like, if you want, whatever. It's a high school kid sitting in a mall store. He's like, yeah, if you want to do my work for me, that's awesome. So Alec goes in and cleans up the things, folds the clothes. Just all nice and everything. He comes back and he's like, hey, thanks. Appreciate you let me work. I'll see you later. It took off. And the guys, I'm sure the people that worked there were just like, that's weird. Why did that guy just do all my job for me? So next day Alec came back and he's like, came to the store again. He's like, hey guys, how you doing? All right, good. He's like, hey, do you mind if I help? Clean up again? Like, yeah, if you want. He's got these folding clothes, doing this whole thing. Within about 20, 30 minutes, one of the manager, one of the manager owner, someone came out and was like, what are you doing? He's like, I just love this place. I thought I'd help clean up. He's like, you want a job? He's like, yes, I do. And boom, he got a job that fast. And it came from this, I'm like giving results first. If you look at my timeline, we did coaching for business opportunity. People like five or, I don't know, longer than that, seven or eight years ago. And then we stopped. And for about three year period of time, I went back into the business. And afterwards, after like two or three years, I was like, I want to get back into this business. We kind of want to do the coaching stuff again. And so the first thing I didn't do was make a video and be like, hey, my name's Russell Brunson. I'm so cool. I got tons of results. I'm the most cool guy in the world. He said, last night I'm sitting there flipping through the book and I was like, man, how cool would it be to have Russell come and fix our funnels. I'm like, how much longer until you're done? And he's like, we're pretty close. I'm like, oh my gosh. Crap. OK, this went from a fun little side project to like, oh my gosh, this is a real thing. And so we went in there and we started looking at their current funnels. And there were some things that they weren't doing or whatever. We talked through those. And then I was talking about what are the new opportunities? Like, I was a customer. I bought all your stuff. And then there was nothing else to buy. Like, what else do you guys have? And like, well, we're thinking about this and this and this. And we had this idea for a supplement to be a green drink. I was like, dude, that's it. And I was like, I do supplements. They got a domain name Organify. And then they named it. And they put the other thing. And they put every last penny they had into this supplement. It kept going up from there. And in the last, we're about two, two and a half years since they launched, they done over $25 million with that funnel, which is crazy, right? And so what was cool was afterwards, then Drew made a video of him. And it helps that he's probably the best looking human on earth. And I was like, let's stop. And we got 100 people in our circle. But it wasn't about me pounding my own horn. Like, look how great I am. It was, I got someone to result. My dream client, my person I want to work with. And I put that out there. And what happened is that people like Drew started applying. How many fitness people do we have in this room right now? It's insane. Okay, like Drew's people started applying. Liz's people started applying. Our event used to be predominantly men. Now we've got like half of your women, which is so exciting. That's because Liz's video. And like those things we're putting out there, attracting in our dream clients. But the result is key first. Like getting the result first, and then we can share that. And so the whole concept of doing the Ask Campaign this way is like, let's do a live at the studio, a workshop, do a weekend thing or a six hour thing or three or something that you're going to get some results for people. And it's bringing in a beta group of people. And let's take them through this process so you get results. And some of you guys already have results. Those who don't, like this is where I would start at. I say, next Saturday we're doing a three hour class. I'm going to show you guys how to go from this to this. And we're going to go through this process together and make it free. And you're like, I don't want to do it for free because then we're going to buy in the future. It doesn't matter. Like right now I got to do it for free to get the right people in. So we run this Ask Campaign and say, look, next Saturday I'm going to do a free workshop. I'm going to show you guys how to blank without blank, whatever it might be, what's your number of questions about this thing, fill out a survey and you can come to my workshop for free next Saturday. And get a whole bunch of people in. Get 50 people. Get 100 people to come in and go through this process with you. Whatever it is you teach and as you go through that process with them, then you're going to come out the other side and you're going to have some amazing success stories. And those success stories become the foundation for building the rest of your business. Any business I go into right now, the first thing I'm doing is like, hey, this is a cool product. Let's find some results. Let's go get it free to 100 people if we can. Whatever it is, because those results become the sales message. People always ask me, Russell, what's the future of internet marketing? Like the future of internet marketing is less selling and more like getting results for people and then showing those results. Like that's the easiest sales video ever is actually help someone get a result and then just document and show that. Like that's better than any like copywriting course you could ever go through. You've captured them right. So that's kind of the thing. Now the way these ask campaigns work, and this is even if you have an existing business, it's still anytime you create a new offer, a new product, or do anything or you're stagnant or you can't figure things out like we use these all the time. Like we're bringing them out every time we're trying to figure out the next piece. So it comes through here and you ask them what's the number one question and what will happen is you start getting a whole bunch of different things coming in. So when we did this for my potato gun product initially, I started getting all these questions coming in, right? And I ran out until I had about a hundred questions. So that's kind of my goal is to get a hundred people to submit questions, which means all the hundred people in my beta group, right? So questions start coming through and all sorts of stuff like what kind of pipes should I use? Is the pipes going to blow up? Like what's and like all these things start coming in, right? And after I get a hundred or so, I'll stop the advertising campaign for paying for it. I'll say, okay, here's all the data of like all these people coming through. But if you look at that like traditionally, like most of these things are going to kind of fit into some groups, right? So let's say from that, like, okay, there's, you know, 15 of these questions are all about like what pipes? So I'm going to say, okay, module number one in my course is how to pick the right pipe, whatever that is, right? Okay. And the next one's maybe they're coming in and they're all about something else. So module number two is about what's the right barrel to chamber volume ratio for a potato gun, which all you guys have that mathematical formula now in the very intro of the Expert Seekers book. There's a little sketch with it. That is the mathematical formula in case any of you guys are wondering what it actually is. So if you're making potato guns, now you've got it. So I go through and I figure from a hundred questions, I'm going to get somewhere from six, eight modules and that becomes the structure for my book, for my course, for my event, for whatever it is I'm trying to do. But they told me exactly what it was. They said, I want to learn this. I'm like, cool. Because I covered that in module number one, right? When we come back later, I'm going to sell this. All these questions become like all the sales point for when I'm selling something. Okay. I want to know what's the fastest way to get a hundred people to my website. When I do my sales video, my webinar tomorrow, I'm going to be like, hey, second thing you learn how to get a hundred people, the fastest way to get a hundred people to your website. Like I'm using their questions back on them and it becomes so much easier to sell. Like they're telling you what they want. You just take that, rephrase it and you get those people come back in. So that's what an ask campaign is. These are something I think you guys should, everybody should be running their business at least quarterly. If you have an active business, if you're beginning, this should be like the main focus to get people in to find out exactly what they want because you can offer them new opportunity. But if they get in there and they're like, ah, there's no city player in this car, then they're going to leave. You offer new opportunity, they come in and they're like, oh man, city player's there. It's got to be specific. Like what's your number one question about how to get more people into your funnel?
